We always, a couple of weeks later, we will create a segment that says they received this email with the subject line of the first send, but they never opened the email with the subject line of the first send and resend it with a new subject line. And this allows us to kind of pick up any stragglers and get a little bit more revenue from this blog post and get more people to open up the email. So we don't do that too often. We don't want to be mailing to unengaged people very often, but it is a strategy that we have been following for a very long time and proves to work pretty good for us. So a few email copy notes is subject line is super important. So if you're not focusing on anything else in your email, you want to be focusing on your subject line, doing any tests, any A-B tests, trying to figure out what your audience resonates with the most, especially as we're coming into this kind of cookie list world and tracking for email opens is predicted to go away pretty soon. You want to make sure you are testing now and figuring out what your audience is most likely to open. So subject line is super important. We always want to include pull quotes from videos or our articles actually. We want to make sure we're including little quotes from the articles to entice them to open it up and read. And we want to remember the goal. So our goal on most of these emails, most of these blog emails is to get them to go over and read the blog, right? So we want to make sure we are communicating that in the email itself and we have calls to action. Like I said, we have the button up top, we have linked text itself, the images are linked and we have the button on the bottom as well. So when they are scrolling, if they're on mobile, they see a button right up top and then if they scroll down, they have another chance to head over to the blog as well without scrolling too much, keep it. And the last is keep it simple, right? We want to make sure that our emails are short and precise and this is kind of, this is the rule, but there are exceptions to the rule, right? There are times you're going to want to write a longer kind of email, but as far as the blog posts go and putting out content, we want to keep it short, keep it simple and kind of entice them to click over. And then we have our buttons, our links and our CTAs. We want to make sure we have our links in there and they're going to the right spot.
